strengths: Clear identification of three specific, real-world objects with well-described personal contexts
Consistent links made between each object and the chosen IA prompt (Prompt 25)
Use of concrete anecdotes that provide evidence beyond mere description
Defensible justification for inclusion of each object, supported by relevant examples
weaknesses: Analytical depth is uneven across objects, with the patterned top and dress sections relying on broad statements rather than detailed analysis
Some claims are asserted without sufficient argument or development
Named sources are seldom integrated to deepen the analysis
Occasional lapses in precision and repetition prevent elevation to the 7â8 band
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exhibition meets the requirements for the 5â6 band by clearly identifying objects, situating them in real-world contexts, and linking each to the IA prompt with supporting evidence. Strong personal anecdotes and a coherent thread back to Prompt 25 demonstrate TOK in action. However, uneven analytical rigorâparticularly in the patterned top and dress discussionsâalongside under-used sources and occasional imprecision limit the work from consistently achieving the clarity and depth of the 7â8 band. These factors justify a final score of 6.

annotations.comment: The discussion of ways of knowing remains abstract; the link between these concepts and the volleyball sketch should be clarified to avoid generic assertions.
Reliance on general definitions from Whelan and "Â¿QuÃ© es el conocimiento?" lacks critical engagement; the student should interweave these sources with their own example to deepen analysis.
Consider expanding isolated assertions with further examples or evidence drawn directly from the first object to reinforce coherence.
The student clearly states Prompt 25 at the outset, establishing a framework, but could integrate its wording into the introduction to more tightly frame the subsequent discussion.
The introduction offers a broad description of knowledge, belief and opinion but stops short of linking these distinctions explicitly back to the real-world objects or Prompt 25âs inquiry.
The detailed depiction of the volleyball sketch allows the reader to visualise the objectâs real context, strengthening the authenticity of the exhibition.
The personal anecdote effectively illustrates how procedural knowledge is gained over time, linking practice to understanding in a concrete manner.
The statement that knowledge evolves succinctly captures a key TOK insight, demonstrating conceptual understanding even in its brevity.
The image description is thorough; to strengthen analysis, explicitly link visual features of the top to the concept of opinion formation in the commentary.
The distinction between opinion and knowledge is asserted but unsubstantiated by TOK theory or scholars; citing specific thinkers could add academic rigour.
The bibliography header is clear, but the works cited entries lack consistent formatting and need uniform presentation of author, title, date and access details.
Identifying the top as belonging to a specific friend grounds the discussion in a vivid, personal context, enhancing the objectâs relevance.
The assertion that emotions shape opinions is valid but underdeveloped; integrating psychology research or specific examples would substantiate this claim.
Reflection on the evolution of belief through critical questioning demonstrates a nuanced understanding of belief as dynamic within TOK.
The conclusion synthesises how perception and belief shape actions, reinforcing the coherence of the three-object narrative.
The contrast in opinions is described, but the student misses an opportunity to analyse how the topâs pattern or style specifically elicits these subjective responses.
General statements about opinions linked to culture and environment lack direct reference to the top example; focus the discussion on the object to avoid abstraction.
This page reiterates definitions without deepening object-focused analysis; consider weaving in reflections on the top to maintain object coherence.
The vivid personal story about wearing the communion dress effectively conveys the significance of belief within the studentâs cultural context.
The definition of belief cites Whitehouse but is not critically applied; the student should analyse how their faith transition embodies TOK concepts.
The referencing style fluctuates (e.g., italics for titles), suggesting the need for consistent academic formatting across all entries.
The image-source notes are useful; consider integrating source acknowledgments within the main commentary to reinforce authenticity.
The comparison between belief and opinion is presented but would benefit from deeper explorationâperhaps contrasting empirical evidence with faith-based reasoning.
Several citations in the works cited are incomplete or inconsistently styled; adopting a single citation standard would improve academic rigor.
The summary revisits earlier definitions without integrating new insights from the objects; consider weaving in object-specific reflections here.
Listing full URLs with access dates enhances transparency and allows verification of secondary sources.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

The exhibition presents
three objects, but the
real-world contexts of
these objects are not
stated, or the images
presented may be
highly generic images of
types of object rather
than of specific
real-world objects. Links
between the objects
and the selected IA
prompt are made, but
these are minimal,
tenuous, or it is not
clear what the student is
trying to convey.
There is very little
justification offered for
the inclusion of each
object in the exhibition.
The commentary on the
objects is highly
descriptive or consists
only of unsupported
assertions.

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.

rubricCriterionStrands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
